steps.title: Use however before the list
steps.content: The clause before the list is complete: “Only five of these dances are included in international ballroom dance competitions.” “However” marks the contrast with the many dance forms mentioned earlier, and a colon can introduce the list of five dances.

steps.title: Introduce the dance list
steps.content: Choice B uses a comma where a colon is needed to introduce the list. Choice C uses a semicolon before a list fragment. Choice D also creates the wrong boundary before the list and fails to introduce it with a colon. The punctuation should be “competitions, however:” before the list. The answer is A.
